items.title: NT Police find body believed to be missing 5yo Alice Springs girl

children.content: Northern Territory Police have found a body believed to be that of a missing five-year-old Alice Springs girl, five days after she disappeared from a town camp.

articles.title: Tributes flow in Alice Springs after 5yo girl's body found

children.content: Tributes are flowing in Alice Springs for a five-year-old Aboriginal girl whose body was found earlier today, as the community mourns her death.

image.altText: Flowers on a fence

articles.title: How to cope with traumatic news - an illustrated guide

children.content: The era of 24-hour news brings traumatic events directly into everyone's lives. Here's how that can affect people, especially children, and some strategies for coping.

gallery.altText: Young girl in pink dress does peace sign with fingers

thumbnail.altText: Young girl in pink dress does peace sign with fingers

avatar.alt: An Australian-Egyptian woman with dark brown shoulder-length curly hair smiling at camera, in a green patterned top.

items.title: NT government accused of hiding long-term watch house detentions

children.content: Just weeks after the CLP government insisted the Northern Territory's watch house crisis was over, the NT's Aboriginal justice agency has accused it of renaming parts of a Darwin watch house to conceal that people are still being held there long term.

image.altText: Low-angle shot of Darwin city watch house building, with blue and white checkered tiles on front of building in view.

items.title: Darwin lord mayor defends proposed 5.5 per cent rates rise

children.content: In his first council budget, Darwin lord mayor Peter Styles has proposed a rates hike which he says he believes people will agree with despite no new major projects being on the cards.

image.altText: Lord Mayor Peter Styles wears a suit and looks sternly past the camera.

items.title: Cases of potentially deadly disease triple in the NT

children.content: Cases of a deadly, vaccine-preventable strain of diphtheria have now risen to 18, after the Centre for Disease Control first declared an outbreak last month.

gallery.altText: A needle injecting a vaccine into a patient's arm.

thumbnail.altText: A needle injecting a vaccine into a patient's arm.

items.title: Fossickers accuse government of breaking promise to 'restore NT lifestyle'

children.content: The Northern Territory government is proposing to introduce fees and permits for recreational fossicking, which hobbyists say breaks a key Country Liberal Party election promise.

image.altText: Two men wearing caps an sunglasses holding mattocks over shoulders

items.title: Why this First Nations film is the must-see movie of the year

children.content: Convinced to return to the story of the 2018 outback drama Sweet Country, Kaytetye filmmaker Warwick Thornton fell in love with the possibilities.

image.altText: A woman sits on the ground in bush Australia, poking at a small fire.

items.title: ASX falls as oil price hits four-year high of $US126 — as it happened

children.content: The Australian share market traded lower as
the oil price surged above $US125 in afternoon trading. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Apologetic insurer claims customers were not tricked in renewal notices

children.content: When the corporate regulator accused RACQ of misleading customers with insurance renewal notices, the insurer apologised. Now, it is defending the notices in court filings.

image.altText: Blue letters spelling RACQ at the top of a white building.

items.title: Private school denies discouraging parents from telling victims about deepfake incident

children.content: The principal of a Tasmanian private school denies claims by two parents that the school ever tried to discourage them from telling their daughters they had been identified in AI deepfake images.

image.altText: School Signage saying "The Friends School"

items.title: From cereal to clothing, see how the price of everyday items has changed

children.content: If it feels like everything is getting more expensive, that's because it is. With the rate of inflation rising this week, we've tracked how the price of everyday items has increased.

image.altText: A group of everyday items; coffee, light bulb, lemon with chart lines

items.title: NZ mosque attacker's appeal rejected in 78-page judgment

children.content: A New Zealand court declines an application by the Australian man who murdered 51 people at two mosques in 2019 to appeal his convictions.

image.altText: A sign showing the Court of Appeal is displayed above a set of clear glass doors on a building.

items.title: 'Forever changed': Parents speak after children killed in house fire

children.content: A statement shared by the parents of two children killed in a Bowen Mountain house fire say their "family's world has been forever changed".

image.altText: Myla Summer-Lea Hughes smiling in a blue shirt, and River William Higgins smiling in a striped orange and blue shirt.

items.title: Artist who taught himself to paint after seeing Roy and HG portrait wins prize

children.content: Sean Layh has won the $3,000 Packing Room Prize at this year's Archibald Prize for his portrait of Jacob Collins, titled The tragicall historie of Hamlet, Prince of Denmarke.

gallery.altText: Portrait of Jacob Collins by Sean Layh; a man laying in a disheveled bed.

thumbnail.altText: Portrait of Jacob Collins by Sean Layh; a man laying in a disheveled bed.

items.title: Man on trial over alleged cocaine smuggling into Port of Newcastle

children.content: Queensland man James Lake-Kusviandy Blee is on trial over a cocaine shipment into Newcastle.

image.altText: A young man with dark wavy hair posing outdoors in soft light.

items.title: Lyon clarifies resignation offer over comment to First Nations players

children.content: St Kilda coach Ross Lyon is proud of the way the club reacted after he offended a group of the club's First Nations players.

image.altText: Ross Lyon looks to his left before an AFL match.

items.title: 20 years after the original, The Devil Wears Prada 2 has something to say

children.content: The Devil Wears Prada 2 drops the beloved (or loathed) characters from 20 years ago into a very different media environment.

items.title: Fed-up residents 'leaving town' due to murky tap water

children.content: When residents of Narrandera, in southern NSW, turn on the tap, the water that comes out is sometimes so dirty it stains their clothes.

items.title: On-duty paramedic stabbed outside cafe in Melbourne's north

children.content: Police have arrested a man who allegedly stabbed an on-duty paramedic in the suburb of Reservoir, in Melbourne's north, before fleeing the scene.

image.altText: A close up image of a uniform paramedic badge which says Ambulance Victoria

items.title: Melbourne teacher charged after camera found in school bathroom

children.content: The 33-year-old was arrested at the school in the city's south-east after the discovery of a camera in a bathroom.

items.title: Music promoter charged with cocaine, cannabis offences

children.content: The former managing director of Hobart events company Vandemonian Touring appears in court charged with trafficking cocaine and possessing cannabis.

image.altText: A crowd faces away from the camera and towards a stage where Bliss n Eso are performing

items.title: Trump's would-be assassin took selfie 'armed to the teeth', court told

children.content: Court documents reveal further details about the case against the alleged gunman at the White House correspondents' dinner in Washington.

image.altText: Cole Tomas Allen taking a selfie in a mirror with various weapons on his person.

items.title: Accused double killer Beau Lamarre-Condon's mother to remain in custody

children.content: The mother of former police officer Beau Lamarre-Condon will spend at least two weeks in custody after she was charged over an alleged attempt to influence a key witness to change their evidence in his upcoming murder trial.

image.altText: Man in blue shirt smiling next to woman in white top

items.title: Mark Latham ordered to pay $100k to MP he sexually harassed and vilified

children.content: The former One Nation MP has been found to have vilified openly gay politician Alex Greenwich in the second case centring around a dispute between the pair.

image.altText: a composite image of Alex Greenwich and Mark Latham

items.title: Blues out to rewrite State of Origin history in Newcastle series opener

children.content: The Blues are yet to enjoy success in Newcastle, a fact they are mindful of ahead of the first Women's State of Origin match against the Maroons.

items.title: Mining prohibited 'in perpetuity' from culturally significant reserve

children.content: Pilungah Reserve, south-west of Mount Isa, has become Queensland's third special wildlife reserve, barring any future mining activity.

image.altText: Red sand dunes

items.title: Emotions run high at inquiry into Victorian bushfires

children.content: A parliamentary inquiry into the devastating bushfires across Victoria over the summer hears the emotional plea of those impacted.

image.altText: a white car has been completely burnt out by a fire

items.title: US charges Sinaloa governor over alleged cartel ties, marking new front in drug war

children.content: The indictment is a significant escalation of US anti-cartel action that is likely to increase tensions between the United States and Mexico.

items.title: Why slick home appliances are actually harder for some to use

children.content: While the newest home technology aims to make life easier, when it prioritises aesthetics over usability, some lives are actually made much harder, writes Nas Campanella.

items.title: Wine code of conduct details emerge as industry continues to shrink

children.content: Industry representatives welcome a new mandatory code of conduct that dictates how wineries treat wine grape growers, but it may be too late for some producers.

items.title: Fuel shock pushes civil contractors to cut jobs and pause works

children.content: Civil construction firms warn surging diesel and material costs driven by the global oil crisis will force lay-offs and project delays, with nearly a third considering winding down.

image.altText: Diesel is pumped into a black ute

items.title: ATSB confirms student and instructor killed in Parafield plane crash

children.content: The ATSB said the plane had been doing circuit training at the airport, but it's not yet known if structured training was underway when it crashed.

items.title: King visits 9/11 memorial as he tours New York

children.content: King Charles III and Queen Camilla met survivors and family members of victims, as well as with New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani.
